preamble: The transformation from a voiced uvular stop to an alveolar flap is a rare example of lenition followed by fronting, typically involving an intermediate uvular fricative stage as seen in some Tibetic and Totonac languages.

examples.note: Reconstructed historical shift where uvular stops weakened and fronted into rhotic liquids in certain lects.
Exists in a state of allophonic variation or free interchange in specific environments.

sources: Hill, N. W. (2010). Personal communication on Tibetic historical phonology.
McFarland, C. D. (1980). A linguistic atlas of the Philippines.
